content: Given that f(x) = 4x^3 - 9x - 14, find the value of f(2).

The cubic

\[
f(x)=4x^3-9x-14
\]

has a rational root at \(x=2\) (substituting we already obtained \(f(2)=0\)).  
Thus \(x-2\) is a factor. Factoring:

\[
4x^3-9x-14=(x-2)(4x^2+8x+7).
\]

The quadratic factor gives the remaining roots

\[
4x^2+8x+7=0\quad\Longrightarrow\quad x=\frac{-8\pm\sqrt{64-112}}{8}
   =-\!1\pm\frac{i\sqrt{12}}{2}
   =-1\pm i\frac{\sqrt3}{2}.
\]

So the only real solution of \(f(x)=0\) is  

\[
\boxed{x=2}.
\]
